title: 'podman info' fails in a Proxmox Fedora nested container
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If I install the COPR repo using the standard Fedora 35 container from Proxmox, the CLI command "podman info" fails.
Alternatively, if I upgrade the container to Fedora 36 using "dnf system-upgrade", the same issue is present.
I was running podman 4.0.2 successfully this way, so suspect the problem is between 4.0.2 and 4.1.0.
This is not a problem under native Fedora 36 Workstation (on my laptop), or under WSL2 (Fedora Remix 36), so it could also be related to my recent Proxmox system update, however this is not an issue with podman 3.4.7 on the same host (see below).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Using the Fedora 35 CT as a base in Proxmox 7.2-4- Create CT
http://download.proxmox.com/images/system/fedora-35-default_20211111_amd64.tar.xz

Run the following commands In the container:
```
# dnf -y update
# dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)'
# dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/podman4
# dnf -y install podman
```



**Describe the results you received:**
```
# podman info
Error: error getting host info: unable to parse user value "": strconv.ParseFloat: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

**Output of `podman info --debug` using the same base Fedora 35 container and podman from the official repo**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body:
Hey Skyblaster, 

by any chance did you also come across any of these errors? 

(setrlimit `RLIMIT_NOFILE`: Invalid argument: OCI runtime error")
(setting rlimits for ready process caused: error setting rlimit type 7: invalid argument: OCI runtime error")
Could you attach the output from your machine of the first few lines.
I think this was fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14241
